source_documents._notes: Copyright 2006 Eli Lilly and Company. Protocol amendment (c).
SAP for CDISC SDTM/ADaM Pilot Project. Authored by Cathy Barrows, Joel Hoffman, Susan Kenny, Sandy Lei, and Arline Nakanishi.

study_identification.study_title: Safety and Efficacy of the Xanomeline Transdermal Therapeutic System (TTS) in Patients with Mild to Moderate Alzheimer's Disease
study_identification._notes_sponsor: Legacy data provided by Eli Lilly. The CDISC SDTM/ADaM Pilot Project team conducted the reanalysis.
study_identification._notes_regulatory: This is a CDISC pilot submission to demonstrate SDTM/ADaM standards for FDA review.

study_design.type: Prospective, randomized, multi-center, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group

treatment_arms.regimen: Two adhesive patches (50 cm2 + 25 cm2) applied daily in the morning, worn 12-14 hours

treatment_arms.dose: 54 mg (50 cm2 active patch + 25 cm2 placebo patch)
treatment_arms.regimen: Two adhesive patches applied daily in the morning, worn 12-14 hours

treatment_arms.dose: 81 mg (50 cm2 active patch + 25 cm2 active patch)
treatment_arms.regimen: Two adhesive patches applied daily in the morning, worn 12-14 hours. All patients started at 50 cm2 TTS then escalated to 75 cm2.

study_design._notes_actual_n: A total of 295 patients were actually randomized: 97 high dose, 98 low dose, 100 placebo.
study_design._notes_stratification: No stratification factors described in Protocol or SAP.

study_periods.description: Visit 1 (Week -2). Informed consent, neuropsychiatric assessment, psychometric testing, medical history, physical exam, vital signs, ECG, chest x-ray, safety labs, CNS imaging review, placebo TTS tolerability test.

Visits 3-12 (Week 0 to Week 24). Randomization at Visit 3. Clinic visits every 2 weeks for first 8 weeks, then every 4 weeks. Efficacy assessments (ADAS-Cog, CIBIC+, DAD) at Visits 3, 8, 10, 12. NPI-X every 2 weeks (clinic or telephone).
Visit 13 (Week 26). End of study visit. Option to enter open-label extension.

modified_itt._notes: No mITT population defined in this study.

per_protocol._notes: No per-protocol population explicitly defined. Completers population serves a similar role.

safety.definition: All patients randomized and known to have taken at least one dose of randomized drug

pk_population._notes: PK analyses not performed in the CDISC pilot submission (SAP Section 15.2).

other_populations.name: Efficacy Population
other_populations.definition: All patients who were randomized and took drug, and have at least 1 post-baseline measure for both ADAS-Cog and CIBIC+
other_populations.purpose: Primary population for all efficacy analyses

other_populations.name: Completers
other_populations.definition: All patients in the efficacy population who completed their Week 24 visit (Visit 12)

primary.name: ADAS-Cog (11) at Week 24
primary.description: Alzheimer's Disease Assessment Scale - Cognitive Subscale, total of 11 items [ADAS-Cog (11)] at Week 24. Change from baseline in ADAS-Cog (11) score. The ADAS-Cog (11) is a standard 11-item instrument used to assess word recall, naming objects, commands, constructional praxis, ideational praxis, orientation, word recognition tasks, spoken language ability, comprehension, word finding difficulty, and recall of test instructions. Score range: 0-70 (higher = worse cognition).
primary.measurement: ADAS-Cog (11) total score, change from baseline

primary.name: CIBIC+ at Week 24
primary.description: Video-referenced Clinician's Interview-based Impression of Change (CIBIC+) at Week 24. Assessment of global clinical status relative to baseline on a 1-7 scale (1=marked improvement, 4=no change, 7=marked worsening). No corresponding baseline score exists; this is a post-baseline only assessment.
primary.measurement: CIBIC+ score at Week 24 (not change from baseline)

secondary.name: ADAS-Cog (11) at Weeks 8 and 16
secondary.description: ADAS-Cog (11) change from baseline at Week 8 and Week 16
secondary.measurement: ADAS-Cog (11) total score, change from baseline

secondary.name: CIBIC+ at Weeks 8 and 16
secondary.description: CIBIC+ score at Week 8 and Week 16

secondary.name: Mean NPI-X Total Score (Week 4 to Week 24)
secondary.description: Mean Revised Neuropsychiatric Inventory (NPI-X) total score from Week 4 to Week 24. NPI-X Total (9) is the sum of individual domain scores (Frequency x Severity) for 9 domains (excluding sleep, appetite, euphoria). Range 0-108. Calculated as mean of all available total scores between Weeks 4 and 24 inclusive.
secondary.measurement: Mean NPI-X total score (9 domains), average of Weeks 4-24

exploratory.name: ADAS-Cog (11) Mean Change from Baseline Over Time
exploratory.description: ADAS-Cog (11) mean and mean change from baseline at each visit (baseline, Week 8, Week 16, Week 24)

exploratory.name: ADAS-Cog (11) by Gender Subgroup
exploratory.description: ADAS-Cog (11) change from baseline to Week 24 analyzed separately for male and female subjects

exploratory.name: ADAS-Cog (11) MMRM Analysis
exploratory.description: Repeated measures analysis of change from baseline in ADAS-Cog (11) to Week 24 using a likelihood-based MMRM model

safety_endpoints.name: Treatment-emergent adverse events
safety_endpoints.description: Incidence of treatment-emergent adverse events by SOC and PT, coded using MedDRA. TEAEs defined relative to first dose (Visit 3/Week 0).

safety_endpoints.name: Treatment-emergent serious adverse events
safety_endpoints.description: Incidence of serious adverse events by SOC and PT

safety_endpoints.name: Dermatological adverse events (time to first event)
safety_endpoints.description: Time to first dermatological event by treatment group, analyzed using Kaplan-Meier methods. Special interest AEs related to transdermal patch application (rash, pruritus, dermatitis).

safety_endpoints.name: Clinical laboratory evaluations
safety_endpoints.description: Hematology and clinical chemistry summarized at Baseline and Weeks 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 26. Includes shift tables, abnormality frequency, and Hy's Law assessment.

safety_endpoints.name: Vital signs
safety_endpoints.description: Blood pressure (supine, standing 1 min, standing 3 min), heart rate (supine, standing 1 min, standing 3 min) at baseline and Week 24/end of treatment. Change from baseline summarized.

safety_endpoints.name: Weight change
safety_endpoints.description: Weight at baseline and Week 24 (with and without early terminations). Change from baseline summarized.

safety_endpoints.name: Extent of exposure
safety_endpoints.description: Average daily dose and cumulative dose at end of study (Week 26 or early termination)

primary_analysis.description: Primary analysis of ADAS-Cog (11) at Week 24 using the efficacy population with LOCF imputation. Test for dose response (treatment as continuous variable).

model_details.response_variable: Change from baseline in ADAS-Cog (11) at Week 24
model_details.other_details: Treatment included as continuous variable for dose-response test. If dose response is significant, pairwise comparisons among 3 groups performed at alpha=0.05. Site*treatment interaction NOT assessed per SAP deviation from protocol.

hypothesis.null_hypothesis: H0: b = 0, where b is the coefficient for treatment (no dose-response relationship)

primary_analysis.multiplicity_strategy: No adjustments for multiple primary endpoints (SAP Section 7.3). Nominal 2-sided p-values reported for each primary endpoint.
primary_analysis._notes_multiplicity_discrepancy: The Protocol (Section 4.3.5) describes a randomization test combining ADAS-Cog and CIBIC+ dose-response p-values to control overall Type I error at 0.05. The SAP deviates from this by making no multiplicity adjustment.

sensitivity_analyses.name: Completers Analysis
sensitivity_analyses.description: Analysis of ADAS-Cog (11) at Week 24 using completers subset (observed data only, no LOCF)

sensitivity_analyses.name: MMRM Analysis
sensitivity_analyses.description: Likelihood-based repeated measures analysis of change from baseline in ADAS-Cog (11) at Week 24. Fixed categorical effects of treatment, site, time (week), treatment-by-time interaction, plus continuous effects of baseline ADAS-Cog (11) and baseline-by-time interaction. Unstructured covariance matrix (Toeplitz if computational singularity).

primary_analysis.description: Primary analysis of CIBIC+ at Week 24 using the efficacy population with LOCF imputation. Test for dose response (treatment as continuous variable).

model_details.other_details: No baseline covariate (CIBIC+ has no baseline value). Treatment as continuous variable for dose-response test. If significant, pairwise comparisons among 3 groups at alpha=0.05.

hypothesis.null_hypothesis: H0: b = 0, where b is the coefficient for treatment (no dose-response relationship)

primary_analysis.multiplicity_strategy: No adjustments for multiple primary endpoints (SAP Section 7.3).

secondary_analyses.description: Analysis of mean NPI-X total score from Week 4 to Week 24 using ANCOVA with baseline score, site, and treatment as independent variables. Treatment as continuous variable for dose-response test.
CMH test for overall differences in CIBIC+ controlling for site. Added at request of FDA reviewers (not pre-specified in protocol).

subgroup_analyses.method: ANCOVA with same model specification, analyzed separately by gender
subgroup_analyses._source: SAP Section 7.2; Tables 12 and 13 in TLG list

interim_analyses.timing: When 50% of patients have completed Visit 8 (8 weeks) and Visit 12 (24 weeks)
interim_analyses.stopping_rules: Method by Enas and Offen (1993) used as guideline for stopping one arm or study for ineffectiveness
interim_analyses._notes: Protocol specified two interim efficacy analyses. SAP Appendix 2 (Section 15.1) states interim analyses will NOT be performed for this pilot submission.

imputation_methods.applied_to: All efficacy endpoints (ADAS-Cog, CIBIC+, NPI-X) — missing postbaseline values
imputation_methods.details: Missing postbaseline efficacy values imputed using LOCF. Based on targeted assessments (the analyzable assessment within assessment windows). Missing baseline values and missing safety data are NOT imputed.

imputation_methods.applied_to: ADAS-Cog (11) and NPI-X total scores when individual items are missing
imputation_methods.details: If >30% of items missing, total score treated as missing. If 1-30% missing, total score adjusted to maintain full scale range (e.g., for ADAS-Cog(11) with 1 item missing out of 11: sum remaining items and multiply by 70/(70 minus max score of missing item)).

baseline_definition.general_rule: Assessments collected at Visit 3 (Week 0, randomization visit) serve as baseline for efficacy measures (ADAS-Cog, CIBIC+, NPI-X).

exceptions.rule: Baseline laboratory values are those collected at Week -2 (Visit 1, screening)
Weight baseline at Visit 3; Height baseline at Visit 1
No baseline CIBIC+ score exists — CIBIC+ is an assessment of change relative to baseline clinical status

visit_schedule.windowing_rules: Assessments assigned to visits as collected on CRFs. ADAS-Cog, CIBIC+, and NPI-X also assigned to visits based on actual visit dates using day = visit date - randomization date. If multiple assessments fall in same window, the one closest to target day is selected. If equidistant, the assessment prior to target day is selected. Retrieval visits (visit 201) included for Week 24 window selection.
visit_schedule._notes_telephone_visits: NPI-X also collected via telephone at Weeks 10, 14, 18, 22 (visit numbers 8.1, 9.1, 10.1, 11.1).

sample_size.assumptions: 90% power to detect 3.0 mean treatment difference in ADAS-Cog (p<.05, two-sided), based on SD=6.5. 80% power to detect 0.36 mean treatment difference in CIBIC+ (p<.05, two-sided), based on SD=0.9. Based on previous experience with oral xanomeline.
sample_size.method: Based on prior oral xanomeline study experience
sample_size._notes_actual: 295 patients actually randomized: 97 high dose, 98 low dose, 100 placebo.

planned_tlg_list._notes: Explicit TLG listing from SAP Section 13.1. 29 tables, 1 figure, 0 listings. Templates provided in SAP Section 13.2.

fields_missing: study_design.stratification_factors — No stratification factors mentioned in either document
source_documents.date — Document dates not available from the PDFs
inconsistencies_found: Multiplicity: Protocol Section 4.3.5 describes a randomization test combining ADAS-Cog and CIBIC+ p-values to control Type I error at 0.05. SAP Section 7.3 explicitly states 'No adjustments for multiple primary endpoints will be made.' The SAP takes precedence.

Efficacy population definition: SAP Section 6 defines it as requiring post-baseline measures for both ADAS-Cog AND CIBIC+. Template 1 note says it includes 'all subjects in the safety population who also have at least one post-baseline ADAS-Cog and CIBIC+ assessment.' These are consistent.
Protocol specifies one-sided tests for pairwise comparisons (Section 4.3.4); SAP Section 7 states all tests are 2-sided at alpha=0.05. SAP takes precedence.
Protocol specifies additional endpoints (ADAS-Cog(14), DAD) and analyses (Apo E, PK, ECG, interim) that SAP Appendix 2 explicitly excludes from the pilot submission.
reviewer_attention_items: SAP deviates from protocol on multiple analyses — see SAP Appendix 2 (Section 15). Key omissions: ADAS-Cog(14), DAD, interim analyses, PK analyses, ECG analyses, Apo E covariate analyses.
CIBIC+ CMH analysis (Table Ad hoc Template 1) was added at FDA request and is not in the original TLG numbering — may need a separate table shell.
No listings are included in the planned TLG list. Typical submissions include subject-level listings (e.g., AE listings, lab listings, concomitant medications listings).
recommendations: Confirm whether the CIBIC+ CMH analysis (SAP Section 16.1) should be added as an additional table in the TLG package.
Consider whether subject-level listings should be added for the submission package.
The SAP uses 'site' (pooled investigator) while the protocol uses 'investigator' — confirm these are the same variable in the analysis datasets.
Review the NPI-X telephone visit handling — telephone visits are included in assessment windows but should be verified for completeness.
